message:
porting(br-1n6f): replace benchmark round shell orchestration with Rust runner

What changed
- Added `conformance_benchmark_runner` as a dedicated Rust binary in `fr-conformance`.
- Moved round-specific benchmark configuration into typed Rust enums (`round1`, `round2`) with explicit output artifact paths.
- Preserved existing benchmark behavior by invoking the same cargo test targets under `hyperfine` and `strace`.
- Converted `scripts/benchmark_round1.sh` and `scripts/benchmark_round2.sh` into thin wrappers that delegate to the Rust binary.

Why
- Removes orchestration logic from ad-hoc shell and centralizes behavior in a testable, typed implementation.
- Keeps benchmark artifact names and command semantics stable while reducing script drift risk.
- Advances the porting-to-rust track by making shell scripts transport wrappers rather than business logic holders.

Behavior + compatibility notes
- Output files remain:
  - `baselines/round1_conformance_baseline.json`
  - `baselines/round1_conformance_strace.txt`
  - `baselines/round2_protocol_negative_baseline.json`
  - `baselines/round2_protocol_negative_strace.txt`
- Wrapper scripts keep `--help` support and forward extra CLI args to the Rust runner.
- Runner emits explicit failure context when `hyperfine`/`strace` return non-zero.

Validation performed
- `cargo fmt --all` and `cargo fmt --check`
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance -- --nocapture`
- `ubs --only=rust crates/fr-conformance/src/bin/conformance_benchmark_runner.rs`

files.patch:
@@ -1,16 +1,24 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 
-mkdir -p baselines
+usage() {
+  cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage:
+  ./scripts/benchmark_round1.sh
 
-hyperfine \
-  --warmup 2 \
-  --runs 5 \
-  --export-json baselines/round1_conformance_baseline.json \
-  'cargo test -p fr-conformance smoke_report_is_stable --test smoke -- --exact --nocapture'
+Description:
+  Thin wrapper around:
+  cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin conformance_benchmark_runner -- --round round1
+USAGE
+}
 
-strace -c -o baselines/round1_conformance_strace.txt \
-  cargo test -p fr-conformance smoke_report_is_stable --test smoke -- --exact --nocapture
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
+  usage
+  exit 0
+fi
 
-echo "wrote baselines/round1_conformance_baseline.json"
-echo "wrote baselines/round1_conformance_strace.txt"
+cmd=(cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin conformance_benchmark_runner -- --round round1 "$@")
+printf 'cmd='
+printf '%q ' "${cmd[@]}"
+echo
+"${cmd[@]}"

@@ -1,16 +1,24 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 
-mkdir -p baselines
+usage() {
+  cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage:
+  ./scripts/benchmark_round2.sh
 
-hyperfine \
-  --warmup 1 \
-  --runs 3 \
-  --export-json baselines/round2_protocol_negative_baseline.json \
-  'cargo test -p fr-conformance tests::conformance_protocol_fixture_passes -- --exact --nocapture'
+Description:
+  Thin wrapper around:
+  cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin conformance_benchmark_runner -- --round round2
+USAGE
+}
 
-strace -c -o baselines/round2_protocol_negative_strace.txt \
-  cargo test -p fr-conformance tests::conformance_protocol_fixture_passes -- --exact --nocapture
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
+  usage
+  exit 0
+fi
 
-echo "wrote baselines/round2_protocol_negative_baseline.json"
-echo "wrote baselines/round2_protocol_negative_strace.txt"
+cmd=(cargo run -p fr-conformance --bin conformance_benchmark_runner -- --round round2 "$@")
+printf 'cmd='
+printf '%q ' "${cmd[@]}"
+echo
+"${cmd[@]}"
